texto: Don’t even talk to me about being a mother. You were never a mother! Just the psychotic twat I lived with for the first fourteen years.
explicacao: Citação de Brandon Shire sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “Don’t even talk to me about being a mother. You were never a mother! Just the psychotic twat I li…” articula familia, bad-childhood, mother em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como mother e motherhood.

texto: Over the years Breece had lectured that truth was liquid. That it evaporated in the heat of passion, froze in the cold of fear, and bent itself around virginous, unpurposeful fibs. It could churn and pull you under, drown you in itself, or let you ride upon it like a surf. But truth was always relfective. It showed blackheads and blemishes, fat rolls and sags, scabs and scars. Truth was fearful, angry and dangerous, and that was why so many people did their utmost to avoid it.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Brandon Shire: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de enfrentar incertezas com ação. O trecho “Over the years Breece had lectured that truth was liquid. That it evaporated in the heat of passi…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, drama, gay-fiction pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de gay-fiction e literary-fiction.

texto: Even now I can feel the heat from your palm as you cupped the back of my head and pulled my lips those last few inches, how you opened your body and begged me with your soul.
explicacao: Citação de Brandon Shire sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Even now I can feel the heat from your palm as you cupped the back of my head and pulled my lips …” articula reflexao, love, sex em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como sex.

texto: The desperate resilience,the annoyed flamboyance;his personable passivity and his phobic aggressiveness; all trapped in the clever wrinkles of his fingers, the hard unsociable cast of his knuckles, the safe hopelessness of the pads.
